positional_arg_descriptor_t(3) Library Functions Manual positional_arg_descriptor_t(3)

NAME

positional_arg_descriptor_t - Positional argument descriptor.

SYNOPSIS

#include <include/ascii-chat/options/builder.h>

Data Fields


const char * name
Name for help text (e.g., address",bind-addr). const char * help_text
Description for usage message. bool required
If true, this positional arg must be provided. const char * section_heading
Section heading for examples (e.g., ADDRESSFORMATS"). const char ** examples
Array of example strings with descriptions. size_t num_examples
Number of examples. int(* parse_fn )(const char *arg, void *config, char **remaining, int num_remaining, char **error_msg)
option_mode_bitmask_t mode_bitmask
Which modes this positional arg applies to.

Detailed Description

Positional argument descriptor.

Describes a positional (non-option) argument parsed after getopt processing. These are arguments that don't start with '-' or '--'.

Examples:

  • Client: "ascii-chat client [address][:port]" - single positional arg with complex parsing
  • Server: "ascii-chat server [addr1] [addr2]" - 0-2 positional args with validation

The parse_fn receives:

  • arg: The positional argument string
  • config: Pointer to full options struct (can modify multiple fields)
  • remaining: Remaining unparsed positional args (for multi-arg parsing)
  • num_remaining: Count of remaining args
  • error_msg: Set on failure (caller will free)

Returns true on success, false on error.

Definition at line 357 of file builder.h.

int(* positional_arg_descriptor_t::parse_fn) (const char *arg, void *config, char **remaining, int num_remaining, char **error_msg)

Custom parser function

Parameters

arg Current positional argument string
Config Module Pointer to options struct (can modify any field)
remaining Remaining positional args (or NULL if last)
num_remaining Count of remaining args
error_msg Set error message on failure (will be freed by caller)

Returns

Number of args consumed (usually 1), or -1 on error
